problem: Working image-generation professionals — concept artists, illustrators, generative-installation artists, retouchers, conceptual / crypto-native creators — need to compose AI image prompts across multiple intentional dimensions (medium, method, subject, style, formal elements, function, context, era) and get an LLM-expanded recommendation without leaving their working environment or depending on a server / API key. Generic prompt boxes ignore the structure that working pros actually compose along and don't expose generation as a tactile, parameter-driven instrument.
stakeholders: Primary: working image-generation professionals (personas include Beeple — daily-throughput pipeline lens; Refik Anadol — installation-scale and dataset-provenance lens; Maciej Kuciara — film concept-art pipeline lens; Erik Johansson — photographic-continuity lens; Pak — mechanism-as-medium / conceptual lens). Skeptic / labor lenses included by design: Greg Rutkowski — illustrator-consent lens; Karla Ortiz — labor and artist-rights lens. Secondary: solo maintainer (Geoff Seemueller), GitHub Pages visitors, the Caretta autopilot agent acting on the repo.
evidence: README.md describes an interactive AI image prompt generator with an 8-axis composition surface and a 3D control panel. src/domain/categories.ts encodes the 8 axes (MEDIUM, METHOD, SUBJECT, STYLE, ELEMENTS, FUNCTION, CONTEXT, HISTORY). src/domain/promptBuilder.ts is the pure composition function; src/domain/promptBuilder.test.ts covers its behavior. src/application/SelectionService.ts owns toggle / randomize / clear / validate. src/application/ports/{ILLMPort,IStoragePort}.ts define the adapter contracts; src/infrastructure/{WebLLMAdapter,LocalStorageAdapter}.ts implement them. src/presentation/components/CortexEnigma renders the synth-style 3D surface. .agents/skills/ holds OpenAI-compatible agent skills (visual-art-framework, art-types-taxonomy, cortex-prompt-axes) migrated from the former references/ folder. .caretta/personas/*.json define seven image-generation-professional personas. CLAUDE.md confirms layered Clean Architecture and deployment to GitHub Pages.
desired_outcome: A static, zero-backend AI image prompt composer where selecting dimensions on a 3D synth-style control surface yields (a) a structured prompt assembled by buildPrompt, (b) an LLM-expanded foundation produced locally via WebGPU, and (c) state that survives reload — deployable as a static site to GitHub Pages and usable by working image-generation pros without leaving their browser.
constraints: Static site only (no server, no API key); base path '/cortex-enigma/'; LLM inference requires WebGPU (Chrome / Edge 113+, excludes Firefox / Safari by default); React 19 + TS 6 + Vite 8 + Three.js stack; layered Clean Architecture (presentation -> application -> domain, infrastructure implements ports); styled-components must be SSR-bundled (noExternal) for prerender; no test suite was configured historically — vitest was recently introduced for domain-layer unit tests only.
dependencies: @mlc-ai/web-llm (Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct via WebGPU), @react-three/fiber + drei + postprocessing, three.js 0.183, styled-components 6, React 19, Vite 8, lucide-react, maath. Dev: vitest 4 (recently added), TypeScript 6, ESLint 9. CI: bun + tsc + prerender. Deploy: GitHub Actions -> GitHub Pages on push to main.
existing_systems: Cortex Enigma itself (src/) — useSelections + SelectionService persist to localStorage; usePromptEngine wraps WebLLMAdapter; CortexCanvas renders the 3D synth scene with knobs / output panel / effects; prerender.mjs SSR-prerenders entry-server.tsx into /dist. .agents/skills/ contains art-compatibility and art-types skills that informed the original 8-axis CATEGORIES vocabulary. Caretta autopilot GitHub Action operates on the repo on schedule / events. No DJ-flavored modules, no MST machine, no Playwright suite are present despite earlier framing — those framings were aspirational and have been dropped.
hypotheses: 1) Structured 8-axis composition produces more useful prompts for working image-gen pros than a single free-text box, because pros already think along these dimensions. 2) A tactile 3D synth surface increases creative engagement vs. checkbox UIs — but only if it stays performant on mid-range laptops. 3) In-browser WebGPU LLM expansion is good enough for ideation despite Llama-3.2-1B's quality ceiling; users will accept the WebGPU browser restriction in exchange for zero API friction. 4) Persona-driven evaluation across pro / skeptic / activist lenses (Beeple throughput vs. Ortiz labor vs. Rutkowski consent vs. Anadol scale) surfaces different blocking issues than generic usability testing.
success_metrics: Successful SSR pre-render and Pages deploy on every push to main (CI green). Vitest domain suite passes (promptBuilder). LLM expansion completes locally without errors. Selection state survives reload. Persona reviews flag no rejection_no_if triggers across the seven image-gen-pro personas — including the consent and labor lenses (Rutkowski, Ortiz), which are the highest-bar reviewers in the cast.
tradeoffs: WebGPU-only LLM = no server cost but excludes Safari / Firefox users. Static GitHub Pages = simple deploy but no analytics or personalization. Layered Clean Architecture adds indirection (ports / adapters) for a small app, but isolates LLM and storage so they can be swapped. Heavy 3D scene + bloom / noise / vignette = visually distinctive but raises GPU baseline and complicates accessibility. The 8 fixed axes match how working pros compose but are inflexible compared to a free-text box — adding new axes is a domain-layer change.
risks: WebGPU compatibility excludes a large share of users. Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct quality may be too weak for nuanced image-prompt expansion (Beeple / Kuciara persona risk). 3D scene + LLM together can OOM mid-range devices. The current CATEGORIES vocabulary is art-history-flavored ('ritual', 'sacred', 'prehistoric') and may read as off-target to the production-pipeline personas (Beeple, Kuciara) — though it matches the art-types-taxonomy skill source material. Consent / training-data origin of the bundled Llama model is a live concern under the Rutkowski / Ortiz personas. Static hosting limits ability to ship hotfixes for runtime LLM issues. Caretta autopilot has write access to the repo — autonomous changes could regress prompt quality.
assumption_summary: We assume working image-gen pros want a structured composition tool (not a generation engine), that WebGPU is acceptable as a hard requirement, that the 8 chosen axes cover the meaningful pro-prompt dimensions, that local Llama-3.2-1B produces useful expansion, and that a 3D synth metaphor is more engaging than a form UI for this audience.
frame_notes: Project frames itself as an AI image prompt generator with a 3D control surface — a composition aid, not a generation engine. The persona cast was recently retargeted from electronic-music producers to working image-generation professionals (plus consent / labor critics), aligning personas with the README and CLAUDE.md framing. The CATEGORIES vocabulary still reflects an art-history source (.agents/skills/art-types-taxonomy) rather than a production-pipeline lexicon — a known mismatch worth deciding on before further work.
risk_notes: No explicit risk acceptance recorded in repo; CLAUDE.md and README acknowledge WebGPU restriction as a known limitation, not a formally accepted risk. The Llama model's training-data provenance has not been audited against the Rutkowski / Ortiz consent bars.

assumptions.evidence: README claims WebGPU works in Chrome / Edge 113+; no telemetry on actual visitor browser share
assumptions.validation_next_step: Add anonymous WebGPU-capability check on page load and log to a static counter or GH-Pages-compatible analytic

assumptions.evidence: Llama-3.2-1B is the smallest practical model; no recorded eval of expansion quality on image-gen prompts
assumptions.validation_next_step: Score 20 representative selection sets through the LLM and rate against persona criteria (Beeple throughput, Kuciara pipeline fit, Anadol scale)

assumptions.evidence: Categories.ts encodes 8 axes; buildPrompt composes them; promptBuilder.test.ts covers buildPrompt behavior
assumptions.validation_next_step: Run persona reviews on sample prompts to confirm axis coverage and address the art-history-vs-production-pipeline vocabulary mismatch

assumptions.evidence: 3D scene with bloom / noise / vignette is visually rich but no perf budget recorded
assumptions.validation_next_step: Lighthouse + WebGPU + three.js perf profile on mid-range laptop and mobile-class GPU

assumptions.evidence: Bundled Llama model's training-data provenance has not been audited against artist-consent criteria
assumptions.validation_next_step: Document model card and training-data origin in README, or evaluate substituting a model with cleaner provenance under the Rutkowski / Ortiz bar

frame_comparisons.framing: Help working image-generation pros compose structured AI prompts across 8 axes with LLM expansion
frame_comparisons.evidence: src/domain/promptBuilder.ts, src/domain/categories.ts, src/infrastructure/WebLLMAdapter.ts wired into CortexEnigma
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Ships now, narrow scope; not a generation engine, not a pipeline tool
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Keep as v1; explicitly label as composition aid, not generation engine

frame_comparisons.framing: An AI ideation surface that exports into Photoshop / C4D / Blender pipelines with batch generation
frame_comparisons.evidence: No pipeline integration in current codebase; WebLLMAdapter generates text only, not images
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Matches production-pipeline persona expectations; requires image generation, exports, batch operations, and likely loses the static-hosting constraint
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Treat as v2; do not block v1 deploy

frame_comparisons.framing: A small system whose rules and parameters produce surprising outputs at scale, treating the surface itself as the medium
frame_comparisons.evidence: Persona definitions .caretta/personas/persona-refik-anadol.json and persona-pak.json; 8-axis CATEGORIES as rule set
frame_comparisons.tradeoffs: Drops the production-pipeline framing; broadens conceptual appeal but loses sharp positioning for working pros
frame_comparisons.recommendation: Hold; revisit if production-pipeline framing fails persona review

decision_log.rationale: No backend cost, simple CI; aligns with zero-API-key in-browser LLM choice
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Server-rendered Next.js app with hosted LLM; Cloudflare Workers AI
decision_log.reversibility: Medium — can move to Workers / Pages Functions later without rewriting domain / application layers

decision_log.rationale: Removes API key, server cost, and per-user latency to a hosted model
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Hosted Claude / OpenAI API; larger local model

decision_log.rationale: Isolates domain logic from React / Three / WebLLM so adapters can be swapped
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Flat React app with inline logic
decision_log.reversibility: Low — refactoring back would lose port boundaries

decision_log.gate: Persona cast retargeted to image-generation professionals
decision_log.rationale: Align personas with the README and CLAUDE.md framing as an image prompt generator; cover production, installation, photo-composite, illustration, conceptual, and consent / labor lenses
decision_log.rejected_alternatives: Retain the original electronic-music producer cast (drifted from the actual product); use a single uniform pro persona (loses the skeptic / labor lens)
decision_log.reversibility: High — persona files are local JSON; previous cast recoverable from git

risk_dashboard.mitigation: Detect WebGPU, show non-LLM mode (selections + prompt only) with clear browser guidance

risk_dashboard.trigger: LLM output quality fails persona review (Beeple / Kuciara)
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Tune system prompt; consider larger quantized model or fallback hosted endpoint behind feature flag

risk_dashboard.trigger: 3D scene + LLM exceed GPU / RAM on mid-tier device
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Reduce postprocessing passes; lazy-load LLM only on user action; add a 'lite' visual mode

risk_dashboard.trigger: CATEGORIES vocabulary (art-history-flavored) reads as off-target to production-pipeline personas
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Decide whether to retain the art-history lexicon (aligned with art-types-taxonomy skill) or migrate to a production-pipeline lexicon, and run a persona review on the choice

risk_dashboard.trigger: Bundled Llama model's training-data origin fails the Rutkowski / Ortiz consent bar
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Document model card and training-data origin in README; evaluate substituting a model with cleaner provenance

risk_dashboard.trigger: Caretta autopilot ships regression to main
risk_dashboard.mitigation: Require CI green + persona-review gate before merge; keep deploy from main not directly from autopilot branches

dependency_graph.reason: Hooks (useSelections, usePromptEngine) call SelectionService and the LLM port
SelectionService operates on CATEGORIES, types, and buildPrompt
LocalStorageAdapter and WebLLMAdapter implement IStoragePort and ILLMPort
Hooks wire concrete adapters into the React tree
npm run build runs tsc then SSR-prerenders entry-server.tsx to dist/
GitHub Pages workflow runs bun run build on push to main and uploads dist/
GitHub Action with write permissions acts on repo events on schedule
